instruction: Using @stdlib/uuid, write a Hemlock program to use equals(), compare(), and is_nil() to classify relationships between a handful of hard-coded UUID strings.
What imports do I need from @stdlib/strings to truncate a list of article titles to 30 characters with '...' suffix and print each? Write the full Hemlock program.
Write a Hemlock program to compute the arithmetic mean and population standard deviation of a list of numbers and print each rounded to 2 decimals. Use @stdlib/math.
Show the imports and Hemlock code to use replace_all with REG_EXTENDED to collapse runs of spaces into a single space and to replace every digit run with 'NUM', then print both results.
Show the imports and Hemlock code to count word occurrences in a space-separated string using a hash map, then print each word and its count.
Solve this in Hemlock: truncate a list of article titles to 30 characters with '...' suffix and print each.
In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/math), seed the RNG to 42, roll ten six-sided dice using floor(rand_range(1.0, 7.0)), print the rolls comma-separated, and print the sum.
What imports do I need from @stdlib/toml to parse a TOML config with a nested [app.limits] table and read four dotted paths using get()? Write the full Hemlock program.

In Hemlock, parse a compact JSON config string and print it pretty-printed with 2-space indentation.
In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/math), print floor, ceil, round, and trunc of a handful of positive and negative decimals so the difference between the four modes is visible.
Solve this in Hemlock: use test() with REG_EXTENDED to validate a few candidate email addresses against a pattern and print true/false for each.
Show the imports and Hemlock code to parse a CSV string into rows, print each row joined with ' | ', then re-serialize with stringify().
Show the imports and Hemlock code to normalize a handful of messy paths (redundant slashes, '..' and '.') using normalize() and also print is_absolute() for each.
Show the imports and Hemlock code to starting from 2026-01-01, compute dates +7 days, +30 days, and +365 days using add_days, then print diff_days between first and last.

In Hemlock, build a playlist in a LinkedList using append, prepend, and insert; iterate by index to print each item; print contains/index_of checks; then remove the head, reverse in place, and print the final order.

What imports do I need from @stdlib/args to parse a synthetic argv like ['script.hml', 'build', '--verbose', '--output', 'out.txt', 'src/main.hml'] and print the flag, an option with default, and the positionals? Write the full Hemlock program.
Write a Hemlock program to check whether a list of versions satisfies the caret range '^1.2.0' and then print patch/minor/major increments of 1.2.3. Use @stdlib/semver.
Solve this in Hemlock: parse a full filesystem path into {root, dir, base, name, ext} components, print each field, and reassemble via format().

What imports do I need from @stdlib/testing to build a tiny test suite with two describe() blocks covering arithmetic and strings, using expect(x).to_equal(y), and call run() to print the report? Write the full Hemlock program.

In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/math), clamp a list of sensor readings into the range [0, 100] and print each clamped value, tracking min/max of the raw readings in the same loop and printing the raw range and clamp range at the end.
Using @stdlib/matrix, write a Hemlock program to compute and print det() and trace() for a 2x2 and a 3x3 matrix constructed via from_rows().
Write a Hemlock program to count word occurrences in a space-separated string using a hash map, then print each word and its count.

In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/collections), build a LinkedList, Queue, and Stack; call to_array() on each and pipe the result through array sort/map/filter/reverse, printing each transformed result.
What imports do I need from @stdlib/math to print cos(a) and sin(a) at five angles around the unit circle (0, PI/4, PI/2, PI, 3PI/2), each formatted to 4 decimals? Write the full Hemlock program.

Using @stdlib/random, write a Hemlock program to set_seed(12345) and print five randint(1,100) values, a choice from a color list, and a sample of 3.
Write a Hemlock program to compute and print det() and trace() for a 2x2 and a 3x3 matrix constructed via from_rows().

Solve this in Hemlock: clamp a list of sensor readings into the range [0, 100] and print each clamped value, tracking min/max of the raw readings in the same loop and printing the raw range and clamp range at the end.
Write a Hemlock program to shuffle a deck [1..10] with set_seed(7), then reshuffle a fresh copy with the same seed and verify the outputs are identical.
Solve this in Hemlock: normalize a handful of messy paths (redundant slashes, '..' and '.') using normalize() and also print is_absolute() for each.
In Hemlock, parse a query string with parse_query(), print each name/value, then use get_query_param and set_query_param on a full URL.
Solve this in Hemlock: parse several ISO-8601 timestamps with parse_iso() and print each as 'weekday month-name time'.
Show the imports and Hemlock code to split a ten-element array into chunks of 3 with chunk() and print each group, then take() the first 5 elements.
Solve this in Hemlock: check whether a list of versions satisfies the caret range '^1.2.0' and then print patch/minor/major increments of 1.2.3.
Show the imports and Hemlock code to parse three version strings into objects, print their components, then use compare() to order a few pairs.
Solve this in Hemlock: starting from 2026-01-01, compute dates +7 days, +30 days, and +365 days using add_days, then print diff_days between first and last.
Using @stdlib/encoding, write a Hemlock program to url-encode a list of query-parameter key/value pairs and print them joined as a single URL query string.
In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/arena), create a 1024-byte Arena, perform three allocations (alloc, alloc, alloc_aligned), print used/capacity/remaining, reset, and destroy.
In Hemlock, hex-encode and hex-decode a few strings and print the round-trip for each.
Write a Hemlock program to parse a TOML document with a top-level key and two tables, print a summary, then re-serialize it. Use @stdlib/toml.
What imports do I need from @stdlib/yaml to parse a small YAML config (server block + features list), print a few fields, then re-emit it with stringify()? Write the full Hemlock program.
Using @stdlib/fmt, write a Hemlock program to use percent() to format ratios as percentages (default and 2-decimal precision), then use wrap() to soft-wrap a paragraph to 30 chars.
In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/csv), parse a CSV and sum the values of a numeric column using get_column() and parse_int().

Using @stdlib/collections, write a Hemlock program to use a Stack to check whether several bracket expressions are balanced and print true/false for each.
Write a Hemlock program to parse a CSV string into an array of objects using the first row as headers, then print each record as 'name: score'. Use @stdlib/csv.

Using @stdlib/regex, write a Hemlock program to compile a [0-9]+ pattern once with REG_EXTENDED, then for each log line use regex.find_all() and the match's start/end indices to substring out the first number (or '(none)' if there is none), and free the compiled regex at the end.

In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/arena), allocate one persistent block in an Arena, save() the mark, allocate two temporary blocks, then restore() and print used bytes at each step.
Write a Hemlock program to check whether each of several words is a palindrome by lowercasing and comparing against its reverse.

In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/json), apply three deep updates to a parsed JSON document using set() (nested object field, array index, and a second-level field), then print the re-serialized JSON.
In Hemlock, build two 2x2 matrices with from_rows(), then print each row of their sum (add) and product (mul).
In Hemlock (importing from @stdlib/async), use parallel_map with a 4-worker pool to square each element of a ten-element array, then print the comma-separated squares and their sum.
In Hemlock, convert a u16 port and a u32 IPv4 address to network byte order with htons/htonl, then back with ntohs/ntohl, printing each step in hex.

Write a Hemlock program to parse a nested YAML document and read three dotted paths with get(). Use @stdlib/yaml.
